name: Sierra Almagrera o de La Plata – circular desde Los Lobos
region: Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón
region_intro: Urban hiking trails in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón traverse a region characterized by coastal mountain ranges and varied topography. The landscape features elevated slopes, abrupt profiles, and a succession of medium and small-sized ranges parallel to the Mediterranean coastline. This area includes spectacular cliffs, secluded coves, and small beaches, alongside diverse vegetation such as Aleppo pine forests and esparto grass fields. The region's geological formations also reveal a significant mining heritage, with remnants of old buildings and tunnels. Best urban hiking trails in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón The most popular urban hiking route is Playazo Beach in Vera – Sunrise at Playa de Vera loop from Vera Playa, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This easy route offers coastal views along the beach. Another top favourite among local hikers is Puerto Rey Lagoon – Salar de los Canos loop from Vera Playa, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path. This trail explores the lagoon area, providing a different urban landscape. Local hikers also love the Vera Playa – Pizzeria Rondoro loop from Vera Playa, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail leading through urban and coastal areas, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Hiking in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón is defined by coastal paths, varied urban landscapes, and views of the Mediterranean Sea. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate urban explorations. The routes in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 290 reviews. More than 2,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many urban hiking trails are available in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón?
A: There are 18 urban hiking trails available in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from easy strolls to more moderate walks, often blending coastal scenery with local town exploration.

Q: What do other hikers say about the urban trails in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón?
A: The urban hiking trails in this region are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from 291 ratings. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of natural beauty with historical elements, and the accessibility of many routes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on urban hikes in this region?
A: Urban hikes in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón often feature a unique blend of coastal and semi-arid landscapes. You can expect to find trails that hug the Mediterranean coastline, offering spectacular cliffs and secluded coves, alongside routes that explore the foothills with diverse vegetation like Aleppo pine forests and esparto grass fields. Many trails also pass through or near local towns, providing a mix of natural and urban environments.

Q: Are there easy urban hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, the region offers many easy urban hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. Out of the 18 available routes, 16 are classified as easy. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10306119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vera Playa – Pretty Roundabout loop from Vera Playa&lt;/a&gt;, which is a gentle 3.2 km stroll.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I discover on urban hikes here?
A: Urban hikes in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón offer a mix of natural beauty and historical intrigue. You can explore coastal features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Cristal Views&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Coastal Stretch&lt;/a&gt;. The region's rich mining heritage is also visible, with sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mina La Luna (Sierra Almagrera)&lt;/a&gt; offering a glimpse into the past. Some routes also pass by historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5626857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Torre de los Alumbres&lt;/a&gt; near Playa de los Cocedores.

Q: Are there any urban trails that offer coastal views or beach access?
A: Absolutely. Many urban trails in the region are situated along the coast, providing stunning Mediterranean views and direct access to beaches. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21834844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playazo Beach in Vera – Sunrise at Playa de Vera loop from Vera Playa&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for a coastal walk, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40377614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Rey Lagoon – Salar de los Canos loop from Vera Playa&lt;/a&gt; also offers beautiful lagoon and coastal scenery.

Q: Are there circular urban hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the urban hiking routes in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21834844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playazo Beach in Vera – Sunrise at Playa de Vera loop from Vera Playa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40374551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vera Playa – Pizzeria Rondoro loop from Vera Playa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the urban trails in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations can vary by municipality and protected areas, many urban trails in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón are generally dog-friendly, especially those along coastal paths or through less restricted areas. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash and clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions, particularly in nature reserves or crowded beach areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for urban hiking in this region?
A: The best time for urban hiking in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). The Mediterranean climate ensures mild winters, making it pleasant for walking. Summers can be very hot, so if hiking during June to September, it's best to go early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the urban hiking trails?
A: Given the urban focus of these trails, you'll often find amenities like cafes, restaurants, and pubs in the towns and villages near the trailheads or along the routes themselves. Areas like Vera Playa, Pulpí, and San Juan de los Terreros offer various options for refreshments and meals, making it convenient to combine your hike with a local culinary experience.

Q: How accessible are the urban trailheads by public transport or car?
A: Many urban trailheads in Sierras Almagrera, De Los Pinos Y El Aguilón are easily accessible, especially those located within or close to towns like Vera Playa. Public transport options, such as local bus services, connect some of these areas. For car users, parking is generally available in or near the starting points of these urban routes, though it's always good to check specific locations beforehand.
tips: Not far from here, in the Jaroso ravine, a rich seam of argentiferous galena was discovered in 1838. Thus began a prosperous period of mining extraction in this small mountain range that also became known as Sierra de la Plata. This particular mine, also known as Mina La Luna, in the Chaparral ravine, stands out for having the only existing wooden winch at present in Sierra Almagrera and for also having the oldest fixed extraction steam engine in Spain. .

Mining town built in 1952 to attract labor to work in the Almagrera mines. The town had a small hospital with a specialist in traumatology and even a cinema, with the idea that the workers would have their needs covered and would not require long journeys.
Mine registered in the year 1870 and exploited between 1900 and 1926 by the Sociedad Minera Almagrera.
